title: Changing asset values
summary: 9706 P2 — depreciation methods, disposals, and revaluation of non-current assets.
learningObjectives: Calculate depreciation using straight-line and reducing balance.
Account for disposal of non-current assets.
Show journal entries for each scenario.

simpleExplanation.title: Assets lose value over time
simpleExplanation.summary: Depreciation allocates the cost of non-current assets over their useful life. Disposals remove cost and accumulated depreciation; revaluation adjusts carrying amount to fair value.
steps: Reducing balance: fixed % on net book value each year.
Disposal: clear asset and acc dep; profit/loss on disposal to SPL.
Revaluation: DR asset, CR revaluation surplus (SOFP equity).

flashcards.back: (Cost − Residual value) ÷ Expected life in years.
NBV × % each year — higher charge early years.
Proceeds > NBV — credit to SPL (or loss if reverse).
Equity reserve — not income through SPL (under revaluation model).

sections.content: **Changing asset values** (1.3.2) — show **full disposal T-account** working in exams; label **profit on disposal** clearly.

**Straight-line depreciation** = $\frac{\text{Cost} − \text{Residual value}}{\text{Useful life}}$

**Reducing balance** = Net book value × Depreciation rate %

sections.question: Machine cost $20,000, residual $2,000, life 5 years, straight-line. After 3 years sold for $9,500. Calculate annual depreciation and profit/loss on disposal.
